**Timestamp:** 2026-07-19T00:20:15.861Z

Core completion review found one material integration gap before acceptance:

- **C1 worker complete:** 19-file runtime delivered with `92/92` focused tests, typecheck, lint, formatting, and diff checks green; its slot was released.

- **C1c (core):** add multi-property permit validation, identity-only external target support, and a synchronous runtime-owned target hook that gates Motion props until its provider lease is granted.
- **S2b (scenes):** derive/validate every GSAP-written property, reject unknown writes, and make the artifact destination identity-only for handoff.
- **C2/C3/C4 repairs:** address V2 findings and migrate high-risk Motion targets to the new gated hook after C1c’s snippet.

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-19T00:20:15.869Z

V3 found one more core enforcement defect during the live re-audit: an external handle was selected by handle key but its registered `part` was not compared with the contract part. A correctly signed handle could therefore satisfy the wrong target role.

C1c is adding an exact part/source/property contract check and a `target-contract-mismatch` regression. I1 is already binding the intended parts, but final acceptance remains blocked until the runtime enforces it.

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-19T00:20:15.869Z

C3’s V2 repair is complete and released: host-bound external export, callback replacement cleanup, unique IDs, pointer inertness, narrowed engraving properties, and Motion lease gating now pass 13 focused tests plus static checks. V2 will re-audit before acceptance because the handoff mentions a host-identity remount that may be unnecessary after C1c’s ref-retry fix.

V3 also found a deeper capability-authenticity issue: external resolution found a live record by ID but did not require the exact minted handle object. A copied/tampered object could reuse the ID and broaden or repoint fields. C1c is adding exact object-identity enforcement for host, target, and external handles plus tampering tests.

200: function unsafeApiRequest(request: Request): UnsafeApiRequest | undefined {\r\n 201:   const method = request.method().toUpperCase();\r\n 202:   if (method === \"GET\" || method === \"HEAD\") return undefined;\r\n 203:   const { pathname } = new URL(request.url());\r\n 204:   if (!pathname.startsWith(\"/api/\")) return undefined;\r\n 205:   return { method, pathname };\r\n 206: }\r\n 207: \r\n 208: function observeUnsafeApiRequests(page: Page): UnsafeApiMonitor {\r\n 209:   const inFlight = new Set<Request>();\r\n 210:   const requestsByLabel = new Map<string, UnsafeApiRequest[]>();\r\n 211:   let activeLabel: string | undefined;\r\n 212:   const onRequest = (request: Request) => {\r\n 213:     const unsafe = unsafeApiRequest(request);\r\n 214:     if (!unsafe) return;\r\n 215:     inFlight.add(request);\r\n 216:     if (activeLabel) requestsByLabel.get(activeLabel)?.push(unsafe);\r\n 217:   };\r\n 218:   const onSettled = (request: Request) => inFlight.delete(request);\r\n 219:   page.on(\"request\", onRequest);\r\n 220:   page.on(\"requestfinished\", onSettled);\r\n 221:   page.on(\"requestfailed\", onSettled);\r\n 222:   return {\r\n 223:     inFlight,\r\n 224:     begin(label) {\r\n 225:       expect(activeLabel, \"Replay mutation-capture windows must not overlap.\").toBeUndefined();\r\n 226:       expect(inFlight.size, \"Replay must begin without an earlier unsafe API request in flight.\").toBe(0);\r\n 227:       requestsByLabel.set(label, []);\r\n 228:       activeLabel = label;\r\n 229:     },\r\n 230:     end(label) {\r\n 231:       expect(activeLabel).toBe(label);\r\n 232:       activeLabel = undefined;\r\n 233:       return requestsByLabel.get(label) ?? [];\r\n 234:     },\r\n 235:     dispose() {\r\n 236:       page.off(\"request\", onRequest);\r\n 237:       page.off(\"requestfinished\", onSettled);\r\n 238:       page.off(\"requestfailed\", onSettled);\r\n 239:     },\r\n 240:   };\r\n 241: }\r\n 242: \r\n 243: async function waitForUnsafeApiQuiescence(page: Page, monitor: UnsafeApiMonitor) {\r\n 244:   await expect\r\n 245:     .poll(async () => {\r\n 246:       if (monitor.inFlight.size > 0) return monitor.inFlight.size;\r\n 247:       await page.waitForTimeout(125);\r\n 248:       return monitor.inFlight.size;\r\n 249:     })\r\n 250:     .toBe(0);\r\n 251: }\r\n 252: \r\n 253: async function installReplayObserver(page: Page) {\r\n 254:   await page.evaluate(() => {\r\n 255:     type ReplayTestWindow = Window & {\r\n 256:       __lanternwakeReplayClasses?: string[];\r\n 257:       __lanternwakeReplayObserver?: MutationObserver;\r\n 258:     };\r\n 259:     const state = window as ReplayTestWindow;\r\n 260:     state.__lanternwakeReplayObserver?.disconnect();\r\n 261:     const host = document.querySelector<HTMLElement>(\".voyage-shell\");\r\n 262:     state.__lanternwakeReplayClasses = [];\r\n 263:     const observer = new MutationObserver(() => {\r\n 264:       if (host) state.__lanternwakeReplayClasses?.push(host.className);\r\n 265:     });\r\n 266:     if (host) observer.observe(host, { attributes: true, attributeFilter: [\"class\"] });\r\n 267:     state.__lanternwakeReplayObserver = observer;\r\n 268:   });\r\n 269: }\r\n 270: \r\n 271: async function replayStartCount(page: Page) {\r\n 272:   return page.evaluate(\r\n 273:     () =>\r\n 274:       (window as Window & { __lanternwakeReplayClasses?: string[] }).__lanternwakeReplayClasses?.filter((value) =>\r\n 275:         value.includes(\"stage-scene-start\"),\r\n 276:       ).length ?? 0,\r\n 277:   );\r\n 278: }\r\n 279: \r\n 280: async function expectMutationFreeReplay(page: Page, monitor: UnsafeApiMonitor, label: string) {\r\n 281:   await waitForUnsafeApiQuiescence(page, monitor);\r\n 282:   const beforeState = await persistedMutationState();\r\n 283:   const startsBefore = await replayStartCount(page);\r\n 284:   const replay = page.getByRole(\"button\", { name: \"Replay ceremony\" });\r\n 285:   await expect(replay).toBeVisible();\r\n 286: \r\n 287:   monitor.begin(label);\r\n 288:   await replay.click();\r\n 289:   await expect.poll(() => replayStartCount(page)).toBeGreaterThan(startsBefore);\r\n 290:   await expect(page.locator(\".voyage-shell\")).toHaveClass(/stage-idle/);\r\n 291:   await expect(replay).toBeVisible();\r\n 292:   await waitForUnsafeApiQuiescence(page, monitor);\r\n 293:   const unsafeRequests = monitor.end(label);\r\n 294:   const afterState = await persistedMutationState();\r\n 295: \r\n 296:   expect(unsafeRequests, `${label} issued an unsafe API request.`).toEqual([]);\r\n 297:   expect(afterState, `${label} changed persisted presentation, presence, access, or session truth.`).toEqual(\r\n 298:     beforeState,\r\n 299:   );\r\n 300: }\r\n 301: \r\n 302: function isCeremonyAcknowledgment(request: Request, eventId: string) {\r\n 303:   if (request.method() !== \"POST\" || !request.url().endsWith(`/api/player/${campaignSlug}/viewed`)) return false;\r\n 304:   try {\r\n 305:     const payload = request.postDataJSON() as { eventId?: unknown };\r\n 306:     return payload.eventId === eventId;\r\n 307:   } catch {\r\n 308:     return false;\r\n 309:   }\r\n 310: }\r\n 311: \r\n 312: test.describe.serial(\"Project Lanternwake Phase />\r\n      <div className=\"access-lantern\" data-scene-part=\"lantern\" data-gsap-owned aria-hidden=\"true\">\r\n        <i />\r\n        <b />\r\n      </div>\r\n      <section IDs used by a visible clone are deterministically namespaced; no source/clone duplicate IDs remain.\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-528-\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-529-After initialization or update, the adapter binds each visible clone to PageFlip instance, clone generation, page identity, index, orientation, current-page state, and lifecycle. It registers only deliberate inner cinematic targets with a local page host. A clone copied from an older generation becomes `stale`, its handles are revoked, and it cannot satisfy a current contract. Off-page clones qualify only when the contract explicitly allows them. PageFlip wrapper transforms are never exported to GSAP; a dedicated inner child is required.\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-530-\r\n--\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-532-\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-533-The required integrity fixture contains a hidden React source, current visible clone, stale prior clone, permanent section target, temporary event target, another host with the same part, detached target, zero-box target, and transparent target. Only the declared current visible target may qualify.\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-534-\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md:535:`manual-page-flip` and `programmatic-page-flip` remain deprecated registry records for traceability, reject production playback, and are removed from fake showcase execution. The showcase demonstrates real PageFlip controls and lifecycle labels. `journal-open` likewise remains deprecated in favor of the bounded Journal opening machine.\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-536-\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-537-## 16. Exact scene disposition\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-538-\r\n--\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-593-cleanup result\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-594-```\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-595-\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md:596:Required failure codes include target resolution codes from section 9 plus `ownership-rejected`, `handoff-failed`, and `cleanup-failed`. Diagnostic snapshots show registered host/target/claim counts, instance reachability, and PageFlip lifecycle without storing DOM nodes. The development showcase shows host IDs, instance IDs, exact target counts, claims, conflict simulation, hidden-source simulation, duplicate simulation, fallbacks, reachability, deprecated replacements, and the real PageFlip runtime. Showcase success is diagnostic evidence, not production integration proof.\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-597-\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-598-Never log invitation codes, PINs, passwords, authentication tokens, private event payloads, secret story content, unpublished Creator content, DOM/HTML/text, or raw errors. Opaque identifiers are bounded/redacted using the Phase 1 sanitizer. Production users receive a safe readable fallback and stable controls, never internal diagnostic detail.\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-599-\r\n--\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-605-2. implement provider-scoped host types, registries, context, invocation identity, target handles, and isolation tests;\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-606-3. implement ownership groups, atomic claims, permits, adapters, release/sweep behavior, and tests;\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-607-4. integrate the director, v2 contracts, receipts, final-state handoff, and cleanup;\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md:608:5. implement PageFlip source/clone identity, sanitization, lifecycle, real-showcase behavior, and retire fake curl execution;\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-609-6. migrate the exact production and legacy scenes through one shared API;\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-610-7. migrate Chart/Log, Altar/Inspection, Companion, Quartermaster/Access, and PageFlip in non-overlapping lanes;\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-611-8. update diagnostics, requirement artifacts, roadmap, test plan, and reports;\r\n--\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-629-\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-630-Chapter release, map location reveal, route reveal, artifact award, quest discovery, log insertion, and finale tease each mount or reuse the appropriate host, register semantic targets by stable domain identity, pass external handles where continuity crosses trees, and use a fresh invocation for replay. Phase 3 must not reinvent host, resolver, ownership, PageFlip filtering, handoff, or identity architecture. This record does not authorize implementation of the complete persistent Player progression UI during Phase 2.\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-631-\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md:632:## 21. Phase 4 handoff: Bring the Harbor Alive\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-633-\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md:634:Phase 4 consumes `platform-ceremony`, `gateway`, and `access` hosts; access/login handoff; Motion/GSAP wrapper ownership; explicit library-card targets; invitation and waiting-room host patterns; and dnd-kit ownership during Studio drag.\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-635-\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-636-Library cards keep Motion layout on the outer node and cinematic children inside. Invitation and waiting-room ceremonies are local hosts with safe target contracts. Studio acquires the `spatial-transform` group for dnd-kit only during drag and does not permit Motion or GSAP transforms on that same node. Modern PlayerSignIn and StaffSignIn visuals are Phase 4, not legacy AccessGate scope. Phase 4 may extend scenes but cannot create an independent ownership registry.\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-637-\r\n--\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-703-\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-704-### 23.1 SceneHost and two-host release gate\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-705-\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md:706:Unit tests cover unique registration, duplicate rejection, unique invocation identity, host-local resolution, identical names across hosts, stale invocation, detached cleanup, nested hosts, external handles, outside-host rejection, unmount, idempotent cleanup, simultaneous Player/Quartermaster hosts, and showcase isolation.\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-707-\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-708-The release-blocking integration fixture mounts Host A `map-reveal` / `route-path` and Host B `route-draw` / `route-path`, runs one scene, and proves only that host changes; metrics name the correct instance; cleanup does not affect the other host; target counts are exact; and no claim leaks.\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md-709-\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Do not begin decorative expansion or Rive art integration before target truth, event acknowledgment, phase interruption and replay persistence are correct.\\r\\n498:\\r\\n499:## 20. Evidence references\\r\\n500:\\r\\n501:- **E-01 - baseline/worktree:** Git status, branch/upstream and detached-worktree checks established `4dbe8c0ae2fbab2785d1d3f26b8d7ba33bf56aee` as the audit snapshot and kept the dirty main checkout out of baseline.\\r\\n502:- **E-02 - static graph:** registry, callers, selectors, event mapping, library wrappers, CSS, assets and state/replay paths at the baseline; summarized in the matrix and scene table.\\r\\n503:- **E-03 - unit/component:** 13 files and 44 tests passed across core/director/scenes/opening machine/Rive/Lottie/PageFlip/workspace interactions.\\r\\n504:- **E-04 - assets:** `npm run assets:validate` passed: 3 Lottie JSON, 1 local development Rive binary, local SVG fallbacks.\\r\\n505:- **E-05 - focused browser:** 3 focused animation E2E tests passed; the WebKit-mobile showcase test was skipped. Chromium and WebKit-mobile first-arrival coverage passed.\\r\\n506:- **E-06 - live showcase:** all 36 entries exercised in reduced mode, no logged runtime exception; final metrics GSAP 0, Rive 1, Lottie 2, PageFlip 0, assets ready/no failures.\\r\\n507:- **E-07 - landing:** six required viewports had no horizontal overflow; at 1440x900 stars had zero height and arrival-copy count was two; reduced content remained readable.\\r\\n508:- **E-08 - journal/PageFlip:** live journal had ten scene-part elements and two visible peripheral targets; PageFlip clone architecture was inspected. No actual event target was observed inside the tested clone snapshot.\\r\\n509:- **E-09 - authentication acceptance:** baseline wrong-login request returned 401, but `.form-error` did not appear within 10 seconds.\\r\\n510:- **E-10 - chapter-release acceptance:** authoritative release reached visible seal/parchment/ink stages and exposed replay, but measured 10,081 ms and failed the `<10,000 ms` contract.\\r\\n511:- **E-11 - isolation boundary:** Next root inference attached the detached server to the main database. Port 3100 was stopped, exact audit rows were removed transactionally after a backup, baseline fields/counts were verified, and no further mutation flow was claimed.\\r\\n512:- Rendered command/output evidence: `Codex_Chats/chats/019f7612-5f0c-7941-8ee5-cf9390e1a6ea--audit-animation-system-failures.md` (SHA-256 `7D7E7510A96790F36A1E35256B38F03F41EB62A972F24BA785220CB519C8965B`). Generated runtime artifacts remain ignored under `artifacts/validation/animation-audit/`.\\r\\n513:\\r\\n514:## 21. Limitations and items requiring further runtime confirmation\\r\\n515:\\r\\n516:- The every-event/every-section matrix is a required future test, not a completed runtime claim. Static scoping proves section dependence; it does not identify which duplicate wins in every browser/layout.\\r\\n46:\\r\\n47:## Phase 1 - Animation truth and broken-trigger repair\\r\\n48:\\r\\n49:This is the exact next implementation phase. It fixes P0 truth and lifecycle failures before any decorative expansion.\\r\\n50:\\r\\n51:### Exact scope\\r\\n52:\\r\\n53:1. Define a typed scene-target contract for every registered scene:\\r\\n54:   - required versus optional parts;\\r\\n55:   - local scene-host and instance identifier;\\r\\n56:   - minimum visible and unique counts;\\r\\n57:   - visibility criteria based on connection, rendered box, display, visibility, and effective opacity;\\r\\n58:   - properties and runtime expected to own each target; and\\r\\n59:   - explicit reduced/fallback presentation requirements.\\r\\n60:2. Add a director preflight and typed presentation outcome. `AnimationDirector.play()` must distinguish at least `presented`, `presented-fallback`, `skipped-by-policy`, `aborted`, `missing-required-target`, `interrupted`, and `runtime-failed`. A built or completed GSAP timeline is not automatically `presented`.\\r\\n61:3. Make progression acknowledgment policy explicit in `PlayerExperience`:\\r\\n62:   - mandatory ceremonies are not marked viewed when required presentation fails;\\r\\n63:   - a named, readable accessible fallback may satisfy the policy when the full scene cannot run;\\r\\n64:   - failed mandatory events remain recoverable instead of being silently cleared;\\r\\n65:   - snapshot refresh is not treated as proof of presentation; and\\r\\n66:   - presentation exceptions are logged with event ID, scene, host, mode, and target-integrity outcome without leaking private payload data.\\r\\n67:4. Add a player-safe persisted replay source for the most recent chapter-release presentation. Prefer the existing sanitized progress event/snapshot data and `ViewedCeremony` model; add schema only if the existing records cannot meet the contract after proof. Replay must reconstruct after refresh and must not call a command endpoint or create progression state.\\r\\n68:5. Repair `waitForJournalPhase()` lifecycle behavior:\\r\\n69:   - race each finite wait with abort and a bounded timeout;\\r\\n70:   - ignore or separately classify infinite ambient animations;\\r\\n71:   - validate the expected opening actor and finite animation set;\\r\\n72:   - give missing actors/animations an explicit fallback outcome instead of an unreported pass; and\\r\\n73:   - settle on skip, unmount, route change, and mode change in a deterministic final semantic state.\\r\\n74:6. Establish a single reduced-motion decision surface for the director, Motion, CSS state, PageFlip fallback, Rive pose, Lottie frame/segment, and sound policy. This phase establishes correctness and propagation; Phase 6 performs final experience tuning.\\r\\n75:7. Classify the eight development-only/dead/redundant scene contracts before leaving the phase: `journal-open`, `manual-page-flip`, `programmatic-page-flip`, `chapter-heading`, `prose-ink`, `marker-stamp`, `ship-course`, and `artifact-inspection`. A scene must be connected to a real semantic trigger, retained as an explicit future contract, or deprecated with callers/tests updated. `seal-break` remains a real legacy Quartermaster production call and is not grouped with unreachable scenes.\\r\\n76:\\r\\n77:### Dependencies\\r\\n78:\\r\\n79:- The audit matrix and full audit provide the 28-scene inventory, target names, call sites, reachability, and severity.\\r\\n80:- The presentation-outcome type and acknowledgment policy must be agreed before changing `PlayerExperience` or its API tests.\\r\\n81:- The replay source must use the existing player authorization and event sanitization boundary in `src/domain/visibility.ts`; raw private event payloads must not be exposed.\\r\\n82:- The phase-wait contract must be stable before Phase 3 changes the physical opening or ceremony handoff.\\r\\n83:- A mutation-test database path must be explicitly resolved, recorded, and compared with the canonical database path before any integration/E2E mutation test starts.\\r\\n84:\\r\\n85:### Files likely affected\\r\\n86:\\r\\n87:- `src/animation/core/animation-types.ts`\\r\\n88:- `src/animation/core/visibility.ts`\\r\\n89:- `src/animation/director/AnimationDirector.ts`\\r\\n90:- `src/animation/director/AnimationDirector.test.ts`\\r\\n91:- `src/animation/director/AnimationProvider.tsx`\\r\\n92:- `src/animation/director/scene-registry.ts`\\r\\n93:- `src/animation/scenes/scene-utils.ts`\\r\\n94:- `src/animation/scenes/arrival.scene.ts`\\r\\n95:- `src/animation/scenes/access.scene.ts`\\r\\n96:- `src/animation/scenes/story.scene.ts`\\r\\n97:- `src/animation/scenes/command.scene.ts`\\r\\n98:- `src/animation/journal/opening-machine.ts`\\r\\n99:- `src/animation/journal/opening-machine.test.ts`\\r\\n100:- `src/components/player/PlayerExperience.tsx`\\r\\n101:- `src/app/api/player/[campaignSlug]/events/route.ts`\\r\\n102:- `src/app/api/player/[campaignSlug]/snapshot/route.ts`\\r\\n103:- `src/app/api/player/[campaignSlug]/viewed/route.ts`\\r\\n104:- `src/domain/story.ts`\\r\\n105:- `src/domain/visibility.ts`\\r\\n106:- `src/lib/snapshot.ts`\\r\\n107:- `prisma/schema.prisma` and an ordered migration only if an evidence-backed replay design requires them\\r\\n108:- focused Vitest files and `tests/e2e/animations.spec.ts` / `tests/e2e/acceptance.spec.ts`\\r\\n109:\\r\\n110:### Libraries involved\\r\\n111:\\r\\n112:GSAP, Motion, CSS, Web Animations API, Web Audio policy, React state, Prisma-backed progress/view records, and the existing animation director utilities. No library consolidation is in scope.\\r\\n113:\\r\\n114:### Acceptance criteria\\r\\n115:\\r\\n116:- Each of the 28 registered scenes has an explicit required/optional target contract and reachability disposition.\\r\\n117:- A scene that finds zero required visible targets cannot return `presented`; duplicate required targets outside the declared cardinality also fail preflight.\\r\\n118:- A mandatory `CHAPTER_RELEASED` presentation is not marked viewed after `missing-required-target`, `runtime-failed`, or an unhandled interruption. An explicit readable fallback may return `presented-fallback` and then acknowledge according to policy.\\r\\n119:- Replay is available after refresh from sanitized persisted data, works without forcing the Player into Journal, and produces no command, event, snapshot, audit, or additional viewed mutation.\\r\\n120:- A journal phase with an infinite animation, missing actor, unresolved `finished` promise, abort, or unmount reaches a bounded documented outcome; abort/skip cleanup completes within 100 ms in the unit harness.\\r\\n121:- Browser reduced motion and product reduced mode both resolve to the same no-travel semantic ordering; `MotionConfig` cannot independently re-enable spatial movement.\\r\\n122:- Development-only and redundant scene status is explicit in registry/tests; none is presented as production-complete solely because the showcase can run it.\\r\\n123:- Target-integrity and acknowledgment logs contain identifiers and counts, not private payload content.\\r\\n124:\\r\\n125:### Tests\\r\\n126:\\r\\n127:- Unit: visibility/cardinality checks for detached, zero-box, hidden, transparent, duplicate, and correctly visible targets.\\r\\n128:- Unit: director outcomes for success, fallback, missing required targets, duplicate targets, abort, interruption, skip, reverse, and cleanup.\\r\\n129:- Unit: opening-phase missing actor, no finite animation, infinite animation, never-settling `finished`, timeout, abort, unmount, mode change, and reduced final state.\\r\\n130:- Component: `PlayerExperience` acknowledgment matrix for every director outcome; the mandatory-event failure remains retryable and the fallback path is readable.\\r\\n131:- API/unit: persisted replay returns only sanitized player-visible fields and survives refresh without mutating progression.\\r\\n132:- E2E: chapter release with and without required targets; replay after refresh; replay network log proves no command mutation; reduced-motion semantic order.\\r\\n133:- Run focused tests in independent shards where they do not share the database. One owner runs mutation-dependent Playwright checks after proving database isolation. One owner runs the final `npm run validate` gate.\\r\\n134:\\r\\n135:### Risks\\r\\n136:\\r\\n137:- Conflating event receipt, business success, visual presentation, and viewed acknowledgment could create duplicate replays or stall event polling.\\r\\n138:- A strict target contract introduced before scene hosts exist may initially expose many failures. Use explicit fallback outcomes; do not weaken required targets to make tests green.\\r\\n139:- Persisting duplicate replay data could leak private payload fields or create a new source of truth. Prefer existing sanitized events and snapshots.\\r\\n140:- Director type changes affect every scene and consumer and therefore require an ordered migration, not parallel incompatible edits.\\r\\n141:- Timeout values that are too short can turn slow but valid accessibility modes into failures; values must derive from scene budgets and remain abortable.\\r\\n142:\\r\\n143:### Work that can happen in parallel\\r\\n144:\\r\\n145:- After the presentation-outcome interface is frozen: target visibility/cardinality implementation, journal WAAPI lifecycle repair, and player-safe replay endpoint analysis may proceed in separate path-owned lanes.\\r\\n146:- Unit tests for target integrity and opening-machine lifecycle may be written alongside their respective implementations.\\r\\n147:- A read-only security review of replay payload sanitization may proceed independently.\\r\\n148:\\r\\n149:### Work that must not happen in parallel\\r\\n150:\\r\\n151:- One owner must edit `animation-types.ts`, `AnimationDirector.ts`, `scene-registry.ts`, and shared scene-builder interfaces in dependency order.\\r\\n152:- One owner must edit `PlayerExperience.tsx` while acknowledgment and replay integration are applied.\\r\\n153:- Prisma schema/migration generation and application are serialized under one schema/database owner; do not create a migration unless the existing model is proven insufficient.\\r\\n154:- Database-mutating E2E, the browser session, ports `3000`/`3100`/`3200`, and the final full validation are exclusive resources.\\r\\n155:\\r\\n156:## Phase 2 - Ownership and scene-scoping architecture\\r\\n157:\\r\\n158:### Exact scope\\r\\n159:\\r\\n160:1. Introduce a dedicated `SceneHost`/scene-instance boundary that provides a local root, unique instance ID, declared target contract, and cleanup lifetime. Director queries must resolve inside that host, not the entire Player or Quartermaster root.\\r\\n161:2. Turn runtime ownership from warning-only metadata into enforcement. A rejected claim must prevent the incompatible writer from receiving the node. Motion participates in the same ownership model through explicit wrappers, not only `data-*` attributes.\\r\\n\", \"type\": \"input_text\"}]\r\n11:import { SceneHost, useSceneTargetRegistration } from \"@/animation/hosts/SceneHost\";\n12:import { useOptionalSceneHost } from \"@/animation/hosts/SceneHostContext\";\n13:import type { SceneHostHandle } from \"@/animation/hosts/scene-host-types\";\n27:  \"invitation-ink\": [\"filter\", \"opacity\"],\n101:function AccessHostBridge({ onReady, mode }: { onReady: (host: SceneHostHandle) => void; mode: MotionMode }) {\n102:  const host = useOptionalSceneHost();\n108:      <AccessTarget part=\"invitation\" targetKey=\"access:invitation\" className=\"access-invitation-cinematic\">\n109:        <AccessTarget as=\"i\" part=\"invitation-ink\" targetKey=\"access:invitation-ink\" />\n136:  const accessHost = useRef<SceneHostHandle | null>(null);\n221:      const receipt = await director.play<AccessOperationResult>(\"player-access\", {\n225:        sceneHost: accessHost.current,\n326:      <SceneHost\n333:        <AccessHostBridge onReady={(host) => (accessHost.current = host)} mode={mode} />\n334:      </SceneHost>\n152:  const openingRun = useRef<AbortController | null>(null);\n272:      openingRun.current = controller;\n284:      if (openingGeneration.current !== generation || openingRun.current !== controller) return;\n285:      openingRun.current = null;\n400:    const controller = openingRun.current;\n402:    openingRun.current = null;\n434:      const controller = openingRun.current;\n435:      openingRun.current = null;\n458:    const controller = openingRun.current;\n459:    openingRun.current = null;\n", "type": "input_text"}]

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-19T00:36:17.579Z

FIX NEEDED.

The layout teardown correctly covers opening listeners, EventSource, timers, key listeners, and tracked requests without creating network resources in a layout effect. However:

- An old identity-session request can gate `setReadingReady(true)` against the replacement registry after `sessionId` changes, permitting a stale write.
- The new tests exercise only the registry, not the mounted component.
- The “immediate” timing assertion is tautological because fake timers freeze `performance.now()`.
- The 20-cycle test does not perform 20 component mount/unmount cycles.

The focused suite passes all 12 tests, but honest component-level coverage and a Chromium rerun of `lanternwake-journal.spec.ts` are still required.

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-19T00:44:12.955Z

D4R is re-auditing the revised journal code and tests read-only. I’ll verify the stale-session race and every requested real-component lifecycle case, then run only focused local checks—no browser, server, database, Git mutation, or edits.
